new_terms.context: NVLink 6 doubles bandwidth by implementing bi-directional signaling over the same number of copper cables.
Each electrical lane is one differential pair (DP) consisting of two conductors that carry equal magnitude and opposite polarity signals.
An inverted copy of the local TX must be generated at the local RX for proper echo cancellation.
The scale-up network continues to be rail-optimized with all-to-all connectivity between each GPU and switch ASIC.
Connected to the NVSwitch tray is the system management module (SMM) that comes with a CPU and acts as a host.
There are two possibilities for the number of OSFP cages - either one 1.6T OSFP cage per GPU or two 800G OSFP cages.
Refers to AI-specialized cloud providers like Coreweave and Lambda.
Components used in CPO-based switches instead of pluggable transceivers.
A module providing the laser source for CPO systems.
A networking architecture common in InfiniBand clusters.
Integrated fiber management within a switch box to simplify multi-plane connections.
A virtualized port representation used to calculate cluster scaling limits.
A condition where a network interface goes up and down rapidly.
Refers to GPU clusters where each GPU connects to multiple independent switch planes for higher bandwidth.
Breakout cables that split a high-bandwidth port into multiple lower-bandwidth connections.
The component in CPO (Co-Packaged Optics) responsible for electrical-to-optical conversion.
A network architecture (typically Ethernet-based) that does not use a centralized scheduler for packet routing.
High-speed copper cables with active circuitry for signal conditioning, used for NIC-to-TOR connections.
A device or function used to convert between different SerDes lane speeds (e.g., 200G to 100G).
